body:
**Precondition: do not merge until `comment-api.sepo-preview.xyz` resolves** (Vercel alias on the comment-api project + GitHub App OAuth callback). Sites that set GISCUS_APP_HOST explicitly are unaffected either way.

- `GISCUS_APP_HOST` default → `https://comment-api.sepo-preview.xyz` (the env-aware default in quartz.ts plus the two defensive fallbacks)
- new `SEPO_PREVIEW_API` env → `data-preview-api` passthrough, for symmetry with `SEPO_PREVIEW_DOMAIN`
- README updated

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

comments.body: [vc]: #mwgyxJJW1KSApYa/HtK6YUdxM0XJAm1wRSpgdkIPVEg=: The latest updates on your projects. Learn more about [Vercel for GitHub](https://vercel.link/github-learn-more). | Project | Deployment | Actions | Updated (UTC) | | :--- | :----- | :------ | :------ | | [literature-template](https://vercel.com/codiscover-ai/literature-template) | ![Ready](https://vercel.com/static/status/ready.svg) [Ready](https://vercel.com/codiscover-ai/literature-template/B2epGtu9Zkgs3gCNZTbDxZb7Ttb8) | [Preview](https://literature-template-git-chore-unified-domains-codiscover-ai.vercel.app), [Comment](https://vercel.live/open-feedback/literature-template-git-chore-unified-domains-codiscover-ai.vercel.app?via=pr-comment-feedback-link) | Jun 11, 2026 6:30am |

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 95 | PARTIAL | 6 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Diff is limited to `README.md`, `quartz.ts`, `Comments.tsx`, and `comments.inline.ts`; all are directly tied to the comments host/default and preview API passthrough. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | Uses existing `envValue(...)` config flow in `quartz.ts` and the existing `Options -> data-* -> forwardedKeys` path instead of adding a parallel parser or runtime. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | Adds a distinct `SEPO_PREVIEW_API` env and `previewApi` option rather than overloading `SEPO_PREVIEW_DOMAIN` or `GISCUS_APP_HOST`. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Name config by capability | pass | 6/6 | `SEPO_PREVIEW_API` describes the preview API capability and is documented as the “Preview registry override,” not as a one-off domain migration flag. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Minimal defaults, native escape hatches | pass | 8/8 | Keeps the built-in behavior to a default host plus env overrides: `GISCUS_APP_HOST` still overrides the default, and `SEPO_PREVIEW_API` is only a passthrough. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | partial | 5/7 | README updates the env table and default host, but the explanatory sentence still lists only `SEPO_PREVIEW_BRANCH` and `SEPO_PREVIEW_DOMAIN` as verbatim-forwarded values even though code now forwards `previewApi` too. |

## Notes

- The implementation is compact and follows the existing comments configuration path: `SEPO_PREVIEW_API` is read in `quartz.ts`, typed in `Comments.tsx`, emitted as `data-preview-api`, and forwarded by `comments.inline.ts`.
- Verification limitation: `git diff --check origin/main...origin/pr-29` passed. `npm run check` could not be completed in this runner because dependencies were absent and `npm ci` is blocked by the local runtime (`node v20.3.1`, while the repo requires `node >=22`).
- Smallest useful follow-up: update the README forwarding sentence to include `SEPO_PREVIEW_API`, or otherwise clarify that only branch/domain have the described service-boundary validation contract.

## Findings

- **WARNING:** The docs are slightly behind the new preview API passthrough. The PR adds `SEPO_PREVIEW_API` to the env table and code forwards it as `data-preview-api`, but the README sentence immediately below still says only `SEPO_PREVIEW_BRANCH` and `SEPO_PREVIEW_DOMAIN` are forwarded verbatim. Evidence: `README.md` lines 158-165 and the new forwarding path in `quartz.ts` / `Comments.tsx` / `comments.inline.ts`.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PARTIAL

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `high`_

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->
<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis-head: fcd15feab3e5f146ab126387b36602c8bc47dd09 -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #29 switches the Sepo comments service default to `https://comment-api.sepo-preview.xyz` across the build config, component fallback, and client inline fallback.
- It also adds `SEPO_PREVIEW_API` as a new preview registry passthrough, mirroring the existing `SEPO_PREVIEW_DOMAIN` flow.
- The PR body marks an external merge precondition for the preview domain/OAuth callback; the PR is currently closed and unmerged.
- Reviewers generally agreed the implementation is compact and follows existing patterns, but found two remaining issues.

## Review
The default host migration is complete and the `previewApi` data path is wired consistently. No blocking issues were found, but two warnings remain before this should ship.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Validate `SEPO_PREVIEW_API` before forwarding | WARNING | [`quartz.ts#L144`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/fcd15feab3e5f146ab126387b36602c8bc47dd09/quartz.ts#L144) forwards a raw API base into the browser; current `sepo.js` uses it as a `fetch` base, so relative or scheme-less values can fail at runtime. |
| Document `SEPO_PREVIEW_API` in the forwarding contract | WARNING | [`README.md#L165`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/fcd15feab3e5f146ab126387b36602c8bc47dd09/README.md#L165) still says only `SEPO_PREVIEW_BRANCH` and `SEPO_PREVIEW_DOMAIN` are forwarded verbatim, even though the PR adds `SEPO_PREVIEW_API`. |

## Progress
- Existing top-level rubrics feedback already covers the README forwarding-contract gap.
- Posted one new inline comment on `quartz.ts:144` for the `SEPO_PREVIEW_API` validation concern: https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/pull/29#discussion_r3393747806
- No existing inline comments were minimized, replied to, or resolved.
- `git diff --check origin/main...origin/pr-29` passed.

## Issue Details
<details>
<summary>Validate `SEPO_PREVIEW_API` before forwarding</summary>

**Cause:** The PR reads `SEPO_PREVIEW_API` with `envValue(...)` and forwards it through `data-preview-api`. The served runtime currently trims trailing slashes and calls `fetch()` with that value as the base, so bad URL shapes can silently become browser-relative requests.

**Candidate solutions:** Reuse the `GISCUS_APP_HOST` validation pattern or extract a small shared absolute-http(s)-URL helper for both hosts. If build-time validation is intentionally rejected, document that choice clearly and ensure `sepo.js` validates/falls back before fetching.

**Comments:** Reviewers disagreed here: one treated verbatim forwarding as intentional service-boundary behavior, while another flagged the runtime failure mode. The current served runtime supports keeping this as a warning.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Document `SEPO_PREVIEW_API` in the forwarding contract</summary>

**Cause:** The README table adds `SEPO_PREVIEW_API`, but the explanatory sentence below it still names only branch and domain as forwarded verbatim.

**Candidate solutions:** Update the sentence to include `SEPO_PREVIEW_API`, and mention any URL requirement if validation is added.

**Comments:** This was independently raised by the rubrics and Claude reviews.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
`FIX_PR`: The remaining issues are concrete branch changes that are small enough for an automated fix-pr pass, though the closed PR may need to be reopened or retargeted first.

## Final Verdict
`MINOR_ISSUES`

## Action Items
- [ ] Validate `SEPO_PREVIEW_API` as an absolute http(s) URL before emitting `data-preview-api`, or otherwise enforce the equivalent fallback in the runtime contract.
- [ ] Update the README forwarding-contract paragraph to include `SEPO_PREVIEW_API` and its URL/validation expectations.

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `xhigh`_

## Rubrics Update

No changes were committed to [`agent/rubrics`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/tree/agent/rubrics) from PR #29.

no rubric changes

I read PR #29’s body, comments, review comments, reviews, diff, repo owner metadata, and the existing rubric set. The only substantive feedback was bot/agent-authored, and the human comments were `/review` triggers from `lolipopshock` with `CONTRIBUTOR` association. The candidate lessons are already covered by existing active rubrics like `keep-docs-in-sync` and `validate-preview-contracts`, so I left the rubrics branch unchanged.

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `medium`_

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 96 | PARTIAL | 7 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Diff is limited to `README.md`, `quartz.ts`, `Comments.tsx`, and `comments.inline.ts`, all directly tied to the comments host and preview API passthrough. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The PR reuses the existing env/config flow and extracts `requireAbsoluteHttpUrl(...)` for both `GISCUS_APP_HOST` and `SEPO_PREVIEW_API` instead of adding a parallel parser. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | Adds a distinct `SEPO_PREVIEW_API` env, `previewApi` option, `data-preview-api`, and `forwardedKeys` entry rather than overloading an existing field. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Name config by capability | pass | 6/6 | `SEPO_PREVIEW_API` names the preview registry API override capability, not a one-off domain migration detail. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Minimal defaults, native escape hatches | pass | 8/8 | Keeps the built-in behavior to a default host plus env overrides: `GISCUS_APP_HOST` remains configurable, and `SEPO_PREVIEW_API` is optional. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Validate preview contracts | pass | 7/7 | [`quartz.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/5cce121764b1983927b2bdabf2ec28a21f72f6d2/quartz.ts#L150) validates `SEPO_PREVIEW_API` as an absolute http(s) URL before emitting browser-facing config. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | partial | 5/7 | README adds `SEPO_PREVIEW_API` and the new default host, but the forwarding-contract sentence still names only `SEPO_PREVIEW_BRANCH` and `SEPO_PREVIEW_DOMAIN`: [`README.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/5cce121764b1983927b2bdabf2ec28a21f72f6d2/README.md#L165). |

## Notes

- The current PR head resolves the earlier validation concern: `SEPO_PREVIEW_API` is now validated with the same helper used for `GISCUS_APP_HOST`.
- The only rubric-specific gap is documentation precision around the new preview API field.
- Smallest useful follow-up: update the README forwarding paragraph to include `SEPO_PREVIEW_API` and clarify that it is build-validated as an absolute http(s) URL.

## Findings

- **WARNING:** `Keep docs in sync` is only partially satisfied. The env table documents `SEPO_PREVIEW_API`, but the explanatory paragraph below still says only `SEPO_PREVIEW_BRANCH` and `SEPO_PREVIEW_DOMAIN` are forwarded verbatim, while the code now forwards and validates `previewApi`.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PARTIAL

_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `high`_

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->
<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis-head: 5cce121764b1983927b2bdabf2ec28a21f72f6d2 -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
PR #29 switches the Sepo comments service default from `https://comment-api.sepo.sh` to `https://comment-api.sepo-preview.xyz` across the Quartz config, component fallback, and inline script fallback. It also adds `SEPO_PREVIEW_API` as an optional preview registry override, emitted as `data-preview-api` and forwarded through the existing comments embed path. The current head `5cce121764b1983927b2bdabf2ec28a21f72f6d2` adds shared absolute http(s) URL validation for both `GISCUS_APP_HOST` and `SEPO_PREVIEW_API`, resolving the earlier validation concern. The PR is already merged; the remaining issue is a small documentation mismatch.

## Review
The implementation is compact and follows the existing env-to-options-to-data-attribute pattern. No blocking code issues remain, but reviewers still flagged one documentation gap around the new preview API contract.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Clarify `SEPO_PREVIEW_API` in README forwarding contract | WARNING | [`README.md#L165`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/5cce121764b1983927b2bdabf2ec28a21f72f6d2/README.md#L165) still says only `SEPO_PREVIEW_BRANCH` and `SEPO_PREVIEW_DOMAIN` are forwarded verbatim, while `SEPO_PREVIEW_API` is now also forwarded and build-validated as an absolute http(s) URL. |

## Progress
- Current head validates `SEPO_PREVIEW_API` with `requireAbsoluteHttpUrl`, resolving the prior validation concern.
- Minimized the superseded same-agent inline validation comment as outdated: https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/pull/29#discussion_r3393747806
- No new inline comments were posted because the README issue is already covered in current PR discussion/review artifacts.
- Codex review reported `npm ci`, `npm run check`, and `git diff --check` passed.

## Issue Details
<details>
<summary>Clarify `SEPO_PREVIEW_API` in README forwarding contract</summary>

**Cause:** The README table adds `SEPO_PREVIEW_API`, but the explanatory paragraph below it still describes only `SEPO_PREVIEW_BRANCH` and `SEPO_PREVIEW_DOMAIN` as forwarded verbatim. The code now validates `SEPO_PREVIEW_API` at build time in [`quartz.ts#L150-L152`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/5cce121764b1983927b2bdabf2ec28a21f72f6d2/quartz.ts#L150-L152), so the docs no longer describe the full contract accurately.

**Candidate solutions:** Update the paragraph to include `SEPO_PREVIEW_API` and distinguish its build-time absolute http(s) URL validation from branch/domain service-boundary validation.

**Comments:** Claude and the rubrics review treat this as a warning; Codex downgraded it to INFO/docs polish. It is concrete and low-risk, but because PR #29 is merged it should land as a follow-up docs patch.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
`FIX_PR`: The only unresolved issue is a concrete README correction that is safe for an automated follow-up patch, with humans accounting for the fact that PR #29 is already merged.

## Final Verdict
`MINOR_ISSUES`

## Action Items
- [ ] Update the README forwarding-contract paragraph to include `SEPO_PREVIEW_API` and its build-time absolute http(s) URL validation.

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `xhigh`_

title: Switch the comments service default to the unified domain (merge after aliases exist)
